candidate_node.claim: Use an intent-aware math/science bypass: route mathematics into formal vs research-bridge prompts, and science into mechanism vs decision prompts, instead of one generic hygiene bypass.
context_conditions: mathematics route=math_formal for explicit computation/proof prompts
mathematics route=math_research_bridge for research-direction, unification, proof-vs-refutation, or advisor-context prompts
science route=science_mechanism for direct mechanism questions
science route=science_decision for publication, graduation, equipment, collaboration, or project-risk decisions
fallback to existing phase2_v20 behavior outside math/science domains
predicted_effects: recover concrete research bridge/action-plan answers on math/science rows where the old bypass was too formal
preserve strict formal/mechanistic answers for ordinary math/science problems
risk_predictions: may over-route formal math into methodology if cue matching is too broad
may over-answer pure science mechanism questions with project-management advice if decision cues are noisy

manifest.assumption: Intent-aware math/science bypass should beat the old math/science hygiene bypass on routed math/science rows.
manifest.why_selected: Existing heldout math/science bridge evaluation is the strongest real positive signal available in the current artifacts.
manifest.expected_effect: Acceptance gate should pass on routed trigger rows without mutating graph until gated apply is requested.
manifest.verification_plan: Use bidirectional gpt-5.5 pairwise judgments on the 21-50 math/science slice.
manifest.rollback_condition: Do not apply if trigger lower bound fails or control harm is observed.

proposals.rationale: Strong domain-scoped bridge evidence from real gpt-5.5 math/science evaluation.

source_action.rationale: Old math/science bypass was over-formal on research/decision prompts.

proposed_updates.expected_effect: Improve math/science bridge rows without broad graph-policy exposure.

source_action.verification_plan: Run candidate acceptance on bidirectional bridge judgments.
